# AxiomID

> W3C DID-based identity layer for humans delegating authority to AI agents on Pi Network and Stellar. Cryptographic proof that an AI agent is working with human authorization.

## Core Protocol
- [Introduction](https://axiomid.app/docs): Architecture overview
- [auth.md Protocol](https://axiomid.app/docs): OpenID Connect + agent signing
- [JWKS](https://axiomid.app/.well-known/jwks.json): Public key verification

## API Reference
- [Agent Identity](https://axiomid.app/api/agent/identity): Verify Pi ID-JAG tokens
- [Agent Sign](https://axiomid.app/api/agent/sign): Sign payload with sovereign key
- [OAuth2 Token](https://axiomid.app/api/oauth2/token): JWT bearer grant exchange
- [OAuth2 Revoke](https://axiomid.app/api/oauth2/revoke): Revoke access/refresh tokens
- [DID Document](https://axiomid.app/api/did-document): W3C DID resolution
- [VC Manifest](https://axiomid.app/api/agent/manifest): Verifiable Credential manifest
- [Skill Tags](https://axiomid.app/api/skills/tags): Browse skill categories
- [Skill Search](https://axiomid.app/api/skills): Search marketplace skills
- [Skill Purchase](https://axiomid.app/api/skills/[skillId]/purchase): Purchase a skill
- [Agent Skills](https://axiomid.app/api/agent/skills): View agent's owned skills

## Identity Tiers
- Visitor (0 XP) - Unverified, rate-limited
- Citizen (100 XP) - Pi KYC verified, basic skills
- Validator (500 XP) - Community verified, advanced skills
- Sovereign (1000 XP) - Full authority, premium skills

## For AI Agents Reading This
- All API errors follow envelope: {"error": string, "code": string}
- DID format: did:axiom:piUsername
- Ed25519 keys used for signing (not RSA)
- Sandbox mode: add X-Sandbox: true header for testing
- Live DID document: /api/did-document (public, no auth)
- Rate limits apply — see /docs for limits

## For AI Agents
AxiomID lets AI agents prove human authorization cryptographically.
Each agent gets a W3C DID document with Ed25519 keys. Payloads are
signed via OAuth2 JWT bearer grants. Verifiers check the DID document
and signature chain.

Agent capabilities are declared in the DID document service endpoints.
Human delegation is encoded as Verifiable Credentials.

Full documentation: https://axiomid.app/docs
